func solve(nums1 []int64) bool {
    n := len(nums1)
    if n == 0 {
        return true // Or false, but constraints say n >= 1
    }

    // Calculate parities of all elements
    parities := make([]int, n)
    for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
        // 0 if even, 1 if odd
        parities[i] = int(nums1[i] % 2)
    }

    // Try target parity T = 0 (Even) and T = 1 (Odd)
    for T := 0; T <= 1; T++ {
        possible_T := true
        
        for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
            p_i := parities[i]
            can_achieve_T_at_i := false
            
            // --- Check Option 1: nums2[i] = nums1[i] ---
            if p_i == T {
                can_achieve_T_at_i = true
            }

            // --- Check Option 2: nums2[i] = nums1[i] - nums1[j], j != i ---
            if !can_achieve_T_at_i {
                // We need p_i XOR p_j = T
                // So, required_parity_for_j = p_i XOR T
                required_parity_for_j := p_i ^ T
                
                found_j := false
                for j := 0; j < n; j++ {
                    if i == j {
                        continue
                    }
                    if parities[j] == required_parity_for_j {
                        found_j = true
                        break
                    }
                }
                
                if found_j {
                    can_achieve_T_at_i = true
                }
            }
            
            if !can_achieve_T_at_i {
                possible_T = false
                break
            }
        }
        
        if possible_T {
            return true
        }
    }
    
    return false
}
